site.name: Shovon Mandal
site.title: Lecturer, Department of Computer Science and Engineering
site.affiliation: Northern University of Business and Technology Khulna, Bangladesh
site.aspiration: Seeking M.Sc./Ph.D. research supervision and RA/TA-supported graduate opportunities in secure distributed systems, blockchain-enabled payment security, NFC/IoT transaction security, implementation-oriented computing, and academic workflow automation. My goal is to contribute to a research group through disciplined literature review, prototype implementation, technical writing, teaching/lab support, data organization, and reproducible academic workflows.
site.headline: Prospective M.Sc./Ph.D. Researcher · Secure Digital Payments · Blockchain/NFC Transaction Security · Academic Automation · Hardware-Oriented Computing

about: I am a Computer Science and Engineering lecturer and graduate researcher with a focused academic direction in blockchain-based secure digital payments, NFC-enabled transaction security, secure distributed systems, and implementation-oriented computing.

My research preparation is grounded in thesis-based development, peer-reviewed/accepted publications, and working prototypes. I have explored secure cashless payment systems, blockchain/NFC interaction, IOTA-based transaction demonstration, and decentralized communication concepts.

A distinctive part of my profile is my automation mindset. I build practical systems using Google Sheets, Apps Script, Python, and web technologies to reduce repetitive academic work, support class routine management, improve student communication, and organize assessment/marking workflows.

As a university faculty member, I also maintain interest in hardware-oriented computing, embedded systems, digital logic, computer architecture, and student prototype evaluation. I served as a judge in the Project Showcasing segment of CSE Fest 2025, supporting project-based computing education.
For a prospective supervisor, my profile combines research alignment, teaching experience, implementation ability, academic service, technical documentation, and readiness for RA/TA responsibilities.

highlightCards.title: Why My Profile Fits Graduate Research
highlightCards.text: I bring research publication experience, secure payment prototype development, teaching exposure, and automation-driven academic workflow design.

highlightCards.title: Research Direction
highlightCards.text: Secure digital payments, blockchain/NFC transaction security, decentralized systems, and implementation-based security research.

highlightCards.title: RA/TA Contribution
highlightCards.text: Ready to support literature review, prototype implementation, data organization, experiment documentation, grading, lab sessions, and student mentoring.

highlightCards.title: Implementation and Lab Readiness
highlightCards.text: Comfortable building software, automation, and hardware-connected prototypes using Python, Apps Script, web tools, Arduino/NFC modules, and structured documentation.

projects.title: Blockchain and NFC-Based Secure Cashless Payment Prototype
projects.description: A thesis-supporting secure cashless payment prototype connecting blockchain-based transaction verification with NFC-enabled payment interaction and implementation-oriented security experimentation.
highlights: Implemented Arduino and Python modules for NFC-based transaction processing.
Supported experimental validation of secure cashless payment workflows.
Connected implementation evidence with blockchain and NFC transaction security research.

projects.title: IOTA-Based Payment Website and Decentralized Transaction Demonstration
projects.description: A web-based prototype demonstrating IOTA-supported payment interaction and decentralized transaction flow, supporting interest in distributed ledger systems and transaction communication.
highlights: Developed front-end and backend components for payment demonstration and user interaction.
Linked implementation work to decentralized transaction and node-confirmation research.
Demonstrated applied interest in distributed ledger systems.

projects.title: Academic Routine Management System using Google Sheets and Apps Script
projects.description: A department-level class routine management system built with Google Sheets and Apps Script to support course-offering based routine preparation, faculty/section-wise routine retrieval, protected routine views, and PDF routine downloads.
highlights: Automated section, teacher, and course-code selection from course offerings.
Supported faculty-wise and section-wise routine extraction for academic use.
Included sheet protection, installable trigger support, individual PDF export, and merged PDF workflow.

projects.title: Python-Based Discord Server Management Bot for Academic Communication
projects.description: A Python-based Discord bot designed for academic server management, student verification, role assignment, nickname formatting, access control, reminders, attendance logging, and admin communication.
highlights: Automated student verification and section-based role assignment.
Improved academic communication and student access management.
Used environment-variable based configuration and security-focused repository structure.

additionalProjects.title: Student Management Android Application
additionalProjects.description: Android Studio-based application designed for student information management and routine academic record workflows.

additionalProjects.title: Google Sheets-Based Marking and Academic Management Workflows
additionalProjects.description: Spreadsheet-driven systems used to keep marking, assessment records, and academic management cleaner, more structured, and easier to maintain.

additionalProjects.title: Traffic Light System
additionalProjects.description: Hardware-oriented project demonstrating control logic, digital system behavior, and implementation concepts.

additionalProjects.title: Fire Alarm Integrated with Mobile SIM
additionalProjects.description: A hardware project combining alert functionality with mobile SIM-based communication and notification concepts.
